content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, suite 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
April 6, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-170 
You have requested advice on behalf of Senator John Garamendi 
concerning his duties under the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act. 1/ 
Your letter accurately summarizes the telephone advice I 
provided to you on March 15, 1989. 
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
DMG:KED:plh 
sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
i/ffk~ { 'bt~~~ 
By: 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1/ 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


LANCE H. OLSON 
Law Offices of 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
March 17, 1989 
Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan, Counsel 
Legal Division 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
RE: 
CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE REQUESTED ON 
BEHALF OF SENATOR JOHN GARAMENDI; ADVICE PROVIDED 
3/15/89 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of March 
15, 1989, this letter is being sent to confirm 
telephone advice which you provided to me on that date. 
The telephone advice confirmed previous telephone 
advice you have provided on the same topic. 
However, 
unlike my previous questions, this advice was requested 
on behalf of an identified client, State Senator John 
Garamendi. 
FACTS 
The facts which I provided to you are as follows: 
BRUCE J. HAGEL 
John Garamendi is a State Senator. 
He was re-
LEROY Y. FONG 
elected to that office in November 1988. 
Since January 
ROBERT E. LEIDlGH 
1, 1989, he has filed an FPPC Form 501 stating his 
intent to seek the office of State Controller in 1990. 
Senator Garamendi also wishes to establish a 
OF COUNSEL 
committee to support qualification and/or passage of a 
LLOYD C. CONNELLY, Member 
"state measure" to alter the so-called "Gann Limit" on 
California State Legislature 
state spending. 
This ballot measure committee would be 
controlled by him. 
The ballot measure would likely 
appear on the ballot in one of the two statewide 
elections in 1990. 
300 Capitol Mall. Suite 350 
Sacramento, California 95814 
TELEPHONE: 
442-2952 
FAX (916) 442-1280 
QUESTIONS 
I posed to you the following questions on behalf 
of Senator Garamendi: 


Letter to Kathryn E. Donovan 
March 17, 1989 
Page Two 
1. 
May Senator Garamendi establish and control a ballot 
measure committee of the type described and obtain contributions 
thereto? 
2. 
If so, are the contributions received and expenditures 
made by the ballot measure committee in any way subject to the 
limitations on contributions under the Political Reform Act? 
3. 
If Senator Garamendi establishes and controls such a 
ballot measure committee, must his name appear in the name of the 
committee? 
ADVICE 
You advised me as follows: 
1. 
Senator Garamendi may establish and control a ballot 
measure committee and obtain contributions to the committee. 
2. 
contributions to a ballot measure committee, even one 
controlled by a candidate who may appear on the same ballot, are 
not subject to the contribution limitations of the Political 
Reform Act. 
Such contributions do not in any way count as 
contributions to the controlling candidate, since they are not 
contributed to support that candidate's candidacy. 
Hence, those 
contributions and their expenditure in support of the measure are 
not subject to the limitations which apply to contributions to 
the candidate's candidate committee(s) under the Political Reform 
Act. 
3. 
If Senator Garamendi establishes and controls a ballot 
measure committee, Government Code section 84106 requires: 
The name of any committee shall include or be 
accompanied by the name of any individual, entity or 
other person by which the committee is controlled. 
Consequently, if Senator Garamendi establishes a ballot measure 
committee which he controls, he must comply with the provisions 
of the section. 


Letter to Kathryn E. Donovan 
March 17, 1989 
Page Three 
I would appreciate recelvlng a written confirmation of this 
advice. 
Thank you for your cooperation and prompt assistance. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
cc: 
Senator John Garamendi
